buys.title: 5 BEST Stocks to BUY Now (I'M BUYING)
buys.reason: Nvidia is the picks-and-shovels play on AI infrastructure. Every hyperscaler — Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta — is competing to build out data centres and Nvidia supplies the GPUs they all need. Margins are expanding quarter on quarter and the order book is reportedly full through 2026. The Blackwell architecture has been a step-change in performance per watt and there is no competitor within 2-3 years of matching it.
buys.cautions: Valuation is stretched at 42x trailing earnings — don't chase above $900. A China export restriction escalation remains the key tail risk.

buys.title: Micron Just Proved Everyone Wrong — Here's What I'm Doing
buys.reason: Long-term hold thesis. The next generation of AI models will require 10x the compute of current ones, and Nvidia's CUDA moat means competitors are years behind. Every AI lab in the world is building on CUDA — switching costs are enormous. Even if AMD closes the hardware gap, the software ecosystem lock-in is the real barrier.
buys.cautions: Short-term pullback likely before next leg up — the stock has run hard. Patient buyers only.

buys.title: These Stocks Will Make Millionaires By 2029
buys.reason: Positioned as a core holding for the AI decade. The data centre segment is now 80% of revenues and growing at over 100% year-on-year. Sovereign AI — governments building their own AI infrastructure — is an underappreciated additional demand driver that wasn't in the bull case 12 months ago.

avoids.title: What the HELL Just Happened to the Stock Market?
avoids.reason: Avoiding Nvidia here. The stock has run 80% in 6 months on hype, not fundamentals that have changed materially. Export restrictions to China are a real revenue risk that the market is pricing wrong — China was 20% of revenues before restrictions and that market is now essentially closed. Meanwhile TSMC concentration risk means any geopolitical Taiwan event would be catastrophic for supply.
avoids.risk: China export ban escalation could remove 15-20% of revenue overnight. Fair value closer to $650 on current numbers.

buys.title: Micron Just Proved Everyone Wrong — Here's What I'm Doing
buys.reason: Micron is at the beginning of a multi-year HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) supercycle driven by AI GPU demand. Every Nvidia H100 and B100 requires HBM3 and Micron is one of only three suppliers globally alongside SK Hynix and Samsung. Their HBM market share has gone from near zero to 20%+ in 18 months and they have long-term supply agreements in place. The forward P/E of 12x is absurdly cheap for a company growing revenues 38% year-on-year.
buys.when_to_buy: Current price is attractive — below $130 is a good entry
buys.cautions: Memory is a cyclical industry — if AI capex slows, HBM demand will soften quickly. Don't over-allocate.

buys.title: The Next Tech Boom Is This!! (4 Stocks to Buy Now)
buys.reason: The most undervalued AI pick in the semiconductor space right now. Everyone is focused on Nvidia but the memory layer is equally critical for running large language models — you can't run a frontier AI model without massive HBM bandwidth. Micron is executing well, margins are expanding, and they are gaining share from Samsung which has had quality control issues on their HBM line.

avoids.title: When to Buy MSFT Stock?
avoids.reason: Intel is in a structural decline that is not priced into the stock even at $20. They have lost the process technology lead to TSMC and Samsung, their foundry ambitions are burning billions with no clear revenue path, and AMD continues to take data centre CPU share. The new CEO has made the right noises about cutting costs but the market they once dominated — x86 server chips — is being eroded by ARM-based custom silicon from Amazon, Apple, and Microsoft.
avoids.risk: Foundry losses could continue for 2-3 years. Fair value possibly $14-16 if the turnaround fails.

avoids.title: These Stocks Will Make Millionaires By 2029
avoids.reason: One to actively avoid in the AI era. Intel missed the GPU compute wave entirely, their AI accelerator (Gaudi) has failed to gain traction against Nvidia, and their manufacturing process is now at least one generation behind. The cash burn on the foundry business is unsustainable without US government subsidies continuing — which is a political risk, not a business merit argument.
avoids.risk: Dividend has already been cut. Further capital raises or asset sales possible.

buys.title: When to Buy MSFT Stock?
buys.reason: Microsoft is the safest large-cap AI play available. They own 49% of OpenAI, have integrated Copilot across the entire Office 365 suite which 400 million people use daily, and Azure is growing at 33% while taking cloud share from AWS. The business model is recurring subscription revenue with extremely high switching costs — enterprises don't rip out Microsoft infrastructure. At 29x forward earnings for a company compounding at 13% revenue growth with 36% profit margins, this is not cheap but it's not overvalued either.
buys.when_to_buy: Below $420 is attractive. Current level is fair value.
buys.cautions: OpenAI relationship has become complicated — any falling out would be a sentiment hit. Also watch Azure growth rate — if it decelerates below 25%, the AI premium in the stock comes under pressure.

buys.title: This is What Always Happens Before a Financial Crisis
buys.reason: In a volatile macro environment, Microsoft is the stock to hide in. It has $80bn in cash, generates $70bn+ in free cash flow annually, and has a AAA credit rating — better than most governments. Copilot monetisation is still early; the $30/user/month Copilot licence is being rolled out across enterprise and represents a genuine incremental revenue stream that isn't fully in analyst models yet.
buys.when_to_buy: Core long-term holding — add on any broad market weakness
